title: Section Lane Picker
prompt:
Create a production-ready Section Lane Picker using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original compact navigator chooses between Brief, Evidence, and Next step. The active lane receives a cyan underline that glides beneath the label, while a focused content sentence changes in the same card. The selected section must be announced with words, so the underline is only a supporting cue. Use a light Bento composition with a #F4F5F6 stage, white surface, #ECECEE border, 22px corners, soft neutral shadow, #111111 text, and #4A4A4F supporting copy. Cyan is the only bright accent and no surface may use a gradient.

Use native tab-like buttons in a labelled group with aria-pressed and a polite aria-live message. Click, Enter, and Space must select a lane; preserve a logical keyboard tab sequence and do not depend on page navigation. Animate the underline and content entrance with a spring at stiffness 330 and damping 27, then fade supporting copy over 150ms ease-out. Keep state local and deterministic with no timers, random values, storage, remote data, network calls, or browser-global access. Prefer opacity and transforms.

At 320px, keep all three labels readable without clipping; at detail preview size, use a modest maximum width rather than a full-width navigation bar. Include visible 3px focus rings, AA contrast, clear selected labels, and text alternatives to the cyan marker. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by moving the underline instantly and keeping only a 120ms opacity fade. Return complete client code, a runnable CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML: <nav class="section-lane"><button aria-pressed="true">Brief</button></nav> */
.section-lane{max-width:340px;padding:20px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.section-lane button{min-height:44px;border:0;border-bottom:3px solid transparent;background:#fff;font-weight:800}.section-lane button[aria-pressed=true]{border-bottom-color:#39c6df}.section-lane button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.section-lane *{transition:none}}

demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [a,setA]=React.useState(0);const l=[['Brief','A short orientation for this decision.'],['Evidence','Two sources support the current direction.'],['Next step','Send the small release for review.']];return
